data.summary: Chose Kotlin + Jetpack Compose with ONNX Runtime for on-device depth estimation
data.detail: Built as a native Android app (not Flutter/React Native) using Kotlin, Jetpack Compose (Material 3), and ONNX Runtime for on-device depth inference. The FP16 Depth Anything V2 Base model runs at 518x518 with NO_OPT to avoid conflicts with pre-fused ONNX nodes.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: App architecture: single HomeScreen with image picker, depth estimation, and depth map preview
data.detail: Initial architecture is a single-screen app with HomeScreen (Compose UI) and HomeViewModel (AndroidViewModel). Model is downloaded from HuggingFace via a Gradle task and managed by ModelManager. BitmapUtils handles image preprocessing for the depth model.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Created SbsApplication to share DepthEstimator and ImageProcessor across screens via Application subclass
data.detail: Rather than each ViewModel loading its own model, a shared Application-level singleton holds DepthEstimator, ImageProcessor, and ModelManager. This avoids duplicate model loads and ensures batch and home screens use the same resources.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: ONNX FP16 model requires NO_OPT optimization level due to pre-fused SimplifiedLayerNormFusion nodes
data.detail: The FP16 ONNX model has pre-fused nodes (SimplifiedLayerNormFusion) that conflict with ONNX Runtime's graph optimization passes. Must use OptLevel.NO_OPT to avoid runtime failures.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Use bilateral unsharp mask at half-res to amplify surface micro-detail in depth maps
data.detail: Introduced DepthEnhancer with a half-resolution bilateral unsharp mask pipeline: downsample -> bilateral filter -> upsample -> subtract from original to extract detail, then add back scaled. Half-res optimization works because the bilateral produces a smooth version where fine detail lost by downsampling is irrelevant since it gets subtracted anyway.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Added BatchScreen with multi-image picker, sequential queue, auto-tuned params via DepthAnalyzer, and inline SBS previews
data.detail: Batch processing uses a sequential queue to process images one at a time, with progress tracking and inline result previews. DepthAnalyzer auto-tunes parameters per image. Navigation between Home and Batch uses a simple string-based enum in MainActivity.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Convert depth model from FP32 to FP16, halving size from 376MB to 188MB
data.detail: Added FP16 conversion step to prepare_model.py pipeline. Model goes through: download -> bake fixed shape -> ORT transformer optimization (attention/GELU/LayerNorm fusion) -> FP16 conversion -> copy to assets. Keep IO types as FP32 for compatibility.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Initial commit
data.detail: Bootstrapped the Android SBS stereo image converter app from scratch. The project includes a PRD with full technical plan, a Kotlin/Jetpack Compose UI with a single HomeScreen, ONNX Runtime depth estimation using Depth Anything V2 Base (FP16, 518x518), and a Gradle-based model download task from HuggingFace. Also included Claude Code agent configuration (.claude/ directory with agents, skills, hooks, settings) and test images in assets. At this stage the app can load an image, run depth inference, and display a depth map -- but no SBS stereo output yet.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Screen navigation via string-based enum in MainActivity with rememberSaveable for state preservation
data.detail: Simple navigation pattern using when(currentScreen) blocks instead of a full navigation library. Each screen gets its own ViewModel via viewModel() composable. Survives config changes via rememberSaveable.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add 2% trailing-edge fade ramp in SbsWarper to eliminate black border artifacts from stereo warp
data.detail: When pixels shift laterally for stereo, the trailing edge (left edge for right eye, right edge for left eye) loses pixels, creating black borders. A fade ramp over 2% of image width smoothly reduces the shift to zero at the edge, preventing visible artifacts.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pre-bake ORT transformer graph optimizations at build time instead of runtime
data.detail: Use onnxruntime.transformers.optimizer to fuse attention, GELU, and LayerNorm ops for ViT model (12 heads, 768 hidden) at build time. Runtime only needs BASIC_OPT level. Inference uses 6 threads to target Tensor G5 performance cores.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Reduce default 3D strength from 5% to 2% for more natural baseline stereo effect
data.detail: The previous 5% depthScale default was too aggressive. Lowered to 2% for a more natural starting point. Surface detail slider (0-3 range, default 0.5) was added to let users control micro-detail enhancement independently.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Model loading UI shows phased progress: copy (0-50%) then optimize (50-100%) with status text
data.detail: SbsApplication splits model loading into two visible phases with modelStatusText state flow. Default 3D strength reduced from 2% to 1% for subtler default effect.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add batch processing screen with auto-3D queue and shared model architecture
data.detail: Built the second major screen of the app: a batch processing queue. The key architectural decision was creating SbsApplication as an Application subclass to share the heavy DepthEstimator and ImageProcessor instances across screens, avoiding redundant model loads. The batch screen allows multi-image selection, processes images sequentially with progress tracking, auto-tunes depth parameters via DepthAnalyzer, and shows inline SBS result previews. Navigation was implemented with a simple string-based state in MainActivity rather than introducing a navigation library. HomeViewModel was refactored to consume shared resources from SbsApplication instead of owning them. A loadThumbnail utility was added to BitmapUtils for efficient preview generation. 746 lines added across 9 files.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Redesign HomeScreen from scrollable layout to single-screen with pinned bottom controls and depth reveal slider
data.detail: Replaced the vertically-scrolling layout with a fixed single-screen design: scrollable image preview area on top, pinned BottomControlPanel with sliders and action buttons. Added a depth reveal slider (draggable before/after split), wigglegram preview (auto-alternating left/right eye), and mesh wireframe overlay toggle for visual debugging.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add depth enhancement with bilateral unsharp mask and edge fade
data.detail: Introduced a full depth enhancement pipeline. Created DepthEnhancer class with bilateral unsharp mask (half-res optimization for speed) to amplify surface micro-detail like bumps, folds, and contours. Added gradient-based micro-parallax with edge masking to suppress artifacts at depth discontinuities. SbsWarper gained a 2% trailing-edge fade ramp to eliminate black border artifacts. Default 3D strength was reduced from 5% to 2%. HomeScreen got a new Surface Detail slider (0-3 range) with depth preview overlay that shows enhanced depth in real-time. HomeViewModel tracks dirty state so SBS is lazily regenerated on tab switch rather than on every slider change. The whole pipeline: normalize -> equalize -> gamma -> blur -> bilateral unsharp mask -> warp with gradient micro-parallax and edge fade.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Convert depth model to FP16 and optimize inference pipeline
data.detail: Major optimization pass on the depth inference pipeline. The prepare_model.py script was extended with two new stages: ORT transformer graph optimization (fusing attention, GELU, LayerNorm for ViT architecture) and FP16 conversion via onnxconverter-common. This cut model size from ~376MB to ~188MB. On the Android side, inference threads were bumped to 6 (targeting Tensor G5 perf cores), optimization level set to BASIC_OPT since heavy opts are pre-baked, and XNNPACK EP was removed as incompatible. The loading UX was improved with two-phase progress (copy then optimize) and status text. Default 3D strength was reduced from 2% to 1%.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: OOM crashes from large images and recycled-bitmap crashes from Compose referencing freed bitmaps
data.detail: Large images caused OOM because they were decoded at full resolution. Fixed by adding inSampleSize subsampling and a 2048px cap in loadBitmapFromUri. Recycled-bitmap crashes occurred because asImageBitmap() wraps the original Android Bitmap — recycling it while Compose still draws caused crashes. Fixed by keeping backing bitmaps (sourceBitmap, depthBacking) alive and only recycling after clearing UI StateFlow refs.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Used Canvas.drawBitmapMesh() for DIBR stereo warping instead of OpenGL or pixel-level displacement
data.detail: SbsWarper uses Android's built-in drawBitmapMesh() with a configurable mesh grid (max 256x256) for depth-image-based rendering. Mesh vertices are displaced horizontally based on bilinear-sampled depth values. This avoids OpenGL complexity while still providing smooth, hardware-accelerated warping.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Chose BoofCV SURF for stereo pair feature matching over alternatives like OpenCV
data.detail: BoofCV was selected as a pure-Java/Android library for SURF feature detection and description, avoiding NDK/JNI complexity of OpenCV. Uses cross-checked matching with Lowe's ratio test (0.75 threshold) and stereo vertical disparity filtering to reject outliers.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add histogram equalization and gamma remapping to depth processing pipeline
data.detail: Added equalizeDepthHistogram (CDF-based redistribution) and remapDepthGamma (power curve) to BitmapUtils. Pipeline now runs normalize -> equalize -> gamma remap -> blur -> warp. Also switched to symmetric warping (half disparity per eye) and added convergence point control so users can set which depth appears at screen level.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: 3-pass separable box blur used as Gaussian approximation for depth map smoothing
data.detail: BitmapUtils.blurDepthMap uses 3 iterations of horizontal+vertical separable box blur with a running sum for O(n) performance per pass. Three passes of box blur closely approximates a Gaussian blur.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: RANSAC similarity transform with least-squares inlier refit and symmetric half-correction applied to each image
data.detail: Alignment uses a custom RANSAC loop (500 iterations, 2-point minimal samples) to estimate a similarity transform (rotation, scale, translation). After finding inliers, a least-squares refit on all inliers improves accuracy. The correction is split symmetrically -- half applied to each image -- for balanced output quality.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add mesh-based DIBR warping engine for SBS 3D generation
data.detail: Added the core SBS stereo generation pipeline. Introduced ProcessingConfig (depth scale, blur kernel, parallel/cross-eyed arrangement), SbsWarper that uses Canvas.drawBitmapMesh() with bilinear-sampled depth for per-eye horizontal displacement, an ImageProcessor orchestration layer that normalizes depth, applies blur, and calls the warper, and added normalize/blur utilities to BitmapUtils. The mesh approach divides the image into a grid (up to 256x256), samples depth at each vertex with bilinear interpolation, and shifts vertices left/right to create the stereo parallax effect. This completes the image-to-SBS pipeline: pick image -> depth estimate -> warp -> SBS output.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Batch mode selector pattern: SegmentedButtonRow to switch between AUTO_3D and PAIR_ALIGN modes in BatchScreen
data.detail: Used Material3 SingleChoiceSegmentedButtonRow to let users toggle between batch modes. Each mode has different data models (BatchItem vs BatchPairItem), different image pickers (single multi-select vs sequential pair selection), and different processing pipelines.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Redesign UI with depth reveal slider, wigglegram, and mesh overlay; fix OOM and bitmap lifecycle crashes
data.detail: Major UI overhaul of the HomeScreen. The scrollable layout was replaced with a single-screen design featuring a scrollable image preview area and a pinned bottom control panel. Three new visualization modes were added: (1) a depth reveal slider that lets users drag to compare original vs depth map, (2) a wigglegram preview that alternates left/right eye views at 500ms intervals, and (3) a mesh wireframe overlay showing the actual warp deformation grid. On the processing side, histogram equalization and gamma remapping were added to the depth pipeline to better utilize the full depth range, symmetric warping was implemented (each eye shifts by half the total disparity), and a convergence point slider was added. Two critical crashes were fixed: OOM from loading huge images (solved with inSampleSize subsampling + 2048px cap) and recycled-bitmap crashes from Compose referencing freed Android Bitmaps (solved by keeping backing bitmaps alive and clearing StateFlow refs before recycling). The ProcessingConfig was expanded with depthGamma and convergencePoint fields, defaults changed to depthScale=5, blur=3, arrangement=CROSS_EYED. A new SbsResult model was introduced to carry mesh vertex data alongside the SBS bitmap.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Upgrade depth model from 756x756 to 770x770 (14x55, nearest valid ViT patch multiple to 768)
data.detail: Switched depth model resolution from 756 to 770. 770 = 14x55, the nearest valid ViT patch-size multiple to 768. Updated all references across prepare_model.py, ModelManager, DepthEstimator, BitmapUtils, SbsWarper, and HomeScreen.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add stereo pair alignment batch mode with BoofCV SURF feature matching
data.detail: Introduced a second batch mode (Pair Align) alongside the existing Auto 3D mode. This lets users select stereo photo pairs taken from slightly different positions and automatically aligns them into SBS output. The technical pipeline is: downscale to 768px for detection, SURF feature extraction via BoofCV, cross-checked matching with Lowe's ratio test, stereo vertical disparity filtering (max 25% of image height), RANSAC similarity transform estimation (500 iterations), and least-squares inlier refit. The correction is applied symmetrically -- half to each image -- to minimize quality loss. New models added: AlignmentConfig, BatchMode enum, BatchPairItem. BatchScreen was refactored with a SegmentedButtonRow to switch modes. 855 lines added across 7 files.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Revert bilateral filter to full-resolution and reorder pipeline to enhance before blur
data.detail: Removed the half-res optimization (downsample/upsample helpers) from DepthEnhancer, running bilateral filter at full 770x770 resolution for maximum quality. Also reordered the pipeline from normalize->equalize->gamma->blur->enhance to normalize->equalize->gamma->enhance->blur so surface detail is amplified on unsmoothed depth before blur smooths it.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Upgrade depth model to 770x770 and run bilateral filter at full resolution
data.detail: Upgraded the depth model from 756x756 to 770x770 (14x55, the nearest valid ViT patch multiple to 768). Updated all size references across 9 files including prepare_model.py, ModelManager, DepthEstimator, BitmapUtils, SbsWarper, and HomeScreen. Reverted the bilateral unsharp mask from the half-resolution optimization introduced in the previous commit back to full-resolution processing, prioritizing quality over speed. Also reordered the processing pipeline to run enhancement before blur, so surface detail is amplified on raw unsmoothed depth rather than after blur has already removed fine detail.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Used MediaStore API with IS_PENDING lifecycle for scoped storage save-to-gallery (Android Q+ only)
data.detail: GalleryUtils saves SBS output as JPEG (95% quality) to Pictures/SBS Converter using MediaStore with IS_PENDING=1 during write, then cleared on completion. Explicitly does not support pre-Q devices (returns null). Uses proper cleanup on failure by deleting the partial URI.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Upgrade depth model from 518x518 to 756x756 for higher fidelity depth estimation
data.detail: Switched from depth_anything_v2_vitb_518.onnx to depth_anything_v2_vitb_756.onnx. Updated MODEL_INPUT_SIZE and DEPTH_SIZE constants across DepthEstimator, BitmapUtils, SbsWarper, and ModelManager. Also refactored ImageProcessor to read depth size from DepthEstimator.MODEL_INPUT_SIZE instead of hardcoding 518.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add save-to-gallery utility using MediaStore API
data.detail: Added GalleryUtils, a single-file utility for saving SBS output bitmaps to the device gallery. Uses Android Q+ scoped storage via MediaStore with the IS_PENDING lifecycle pattern to ensure atomic writes. Saves JPEG at 95% quality to Pictures/SBS Converter. This is a standalone utility with no UI integration yet -- that comes in the next commit.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Upgrade depth model to 756x756 and add convergence point control
data.detail: Upgraded the depth model from 518x518 to 756x756 resolution for higher fidelity depth maps. This was a straightforward constant-bump across 5 files — DepthEstimator, BitmapUtils, SbsWarper, ImageProcessor, and ModelManager all had their size constants and model filename updated. The ImageProcessor was also refactored to reference DepthEstimator.MODEL_INPUT_SIZE dynamically instead of hardcoding 518, making future model upgrades easier. Note: the commit message mentions adding convergence point control and arrangement toggle, but those features were actually introduced in the previous commit (12bd3b1) — this commit just handles the model resolution upgrade and the constant cleanup.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Create reusable StereoViewer composable with synchronized zoom/pan for cross-eye 3D viewing
data.detail: StereoViewer splits SBS image into two halves with BitmapPainter, supports pinch-zoom (1-5x), pan, swipe navigation, double-tap reset, and Half SBS mode for AR glasses. Handles portrait vs landscape aspect ratio for correct glasses squeeze behavior.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Added two-stage alignment: RANSAC similarity pass then translation-only refinement using median feature displacement
data.detail: After the initial RANSAC similarity transform, a second pass re-detects SURF features on the cropped output and computes a median vertical displacement correction. This fixes sub-pixel vertical misalignment introduced by the crop step. Horizontal shift is intentionally preserved as parallax.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add ViewerScreen with gallery grid, swipe navigation, delete, and folder browsing of SBS Converter output
data.detail: New 669-line ViewerScreen queries MediaStore for Pictures/SBS Converter folder. Shows thumbnails in grid, opens full-screen stereo viewer with swipe left/right navigation. Images loaded at 4096px max to preserve 2048px per eye. Accessible from Home and Batch screens.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Added black border cropping via computeVisibleCropRect after applying similarity transform to both images
data.detail: After transforming both images, the visible crop rect is computed by mapping source corners through each transform matrix, finding inner axis-aligned bounds for each, then intersecting them. This eliminates black borders from rotation/scale while ensuring both images share the same crop region.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Replace CPU bilateral filter (~13s) with GPU AGSL RuntimeShader two-pass bilateral (<1s)
data.detail: Created GpuBilateralFilter using AGSL RuntimeShader (API 33+) with separable horizontal+vertical passes, KERNEL_RADIUS=42. Depth encoded as grayscale ARGB_8888 and passed via setInputBuffer to avoid color space conversion. Renders via HardwareRenderer+ImageReader pipeline. Falls back silently to CPU on API < 33 or GPU failure.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix pair alignment export to use cross-eye format consistently instead of parallel
data.detail: BatchViewModel changed combinePairSbs from Arrangement.PARALLEL to Arrangement.CROSS_EYED so all output is consistently cross-eye format, matching the stereo viewer expectations.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: ResultPreview was double-inverting SBS halves — export already in parallel format, swap made it wrong
data.detail: The ResultPreview composable was swapping left/right halves of the exported image for "cross-eye validation", but the export was already in parallel format. This double-inversion made the preview incorrect. Fixed by showing the exported image as-is.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add convergence visualization overlay showing blue/red/yellow tint while dragging convergence slider
data.detail: Added a ConvergenceOverlay composable that draws on top of the image preview while the user drags the convergence slider. Blue tint indicates regions that pop out (depth > convergence), red tint for receding regions (depth < convergence), and a yellow contour line at the zero-parallax plane. Sampled at every 4th pixel for performance. Slider tracks adjusting state via onValueChange/onValueChangeFinished callbacks.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: AGSL GPU rendering pattern: RuntimeShader + HardwareRenderer + ImageReader + ARGB_8888 for depth processing
data.detail: Encode float depth as grayscale ARGB_8888, use BitmapShader with setInputBuffer, render via RenderNode/HardwareRenderer/ImageReader, copy HardwareBuffer to software bitmap for pixel readback. This pattern avoids color space conversion issues and works reliably on API 33+.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Cache raw depth map in ViewModel to avoid re-running inference when tweaking 3D settings
data.detail: HomeViewModel caches the raw 518x518 depth FloatArray so users can adjust 3D strength and smoothing sliders and regenerate SBS output without re-running the expensive depth inference. Previous SBS bitmaps are recycled before generating new ones to prevent OOM.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Improve stereo alignment quality and fix pair export format
data.detail: This commit improved the stereo alignment pipeline in two significant ways. First, a black border cropping step (computeVisibleCropRect) was added after the similarity transform to eliminate black regions caused by rotation/scale. It works by mapping source corners through each transform, computing inner axis-aligned bounds, and intersecting both. Second, a two-stage alignment was introduced: after the initial RANSAC similarity pass, a translation-only refinement pass re-detects features on the cropped output and applies a median vertical displacement correction (horizontal is preserved as intentional parallax). A bug was also fixed in ResultPreview where the SBS halves were being swapped for "cross-eye validation" but the export was already parallel format, causing a double-inversion. The preview now shows the image as-is. 236 lines added across 2 files.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Interactive slider feedback pattern: track adjusting state to show/hide real-time visual overlays during drag
data.detail: Used onValueChange to set isAdjustingConvergence=true and onValueChangeFinished to set it false. This pattern lets the UI show ephemeral visual feedback overlays only while the user is actively manipulating a slider, without committing expensive recomputation on every frame.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: All UI elements include tooltips and semantic contentDescription for QA identification
data.detail: Every interactive element (sliders, buttons, images, sections) wraps in TooltipBox with PlainTooltip and includes Modifier.semantics { contentDescription = ... } for accessibility and QA tooling. This is enforced per project conventions in CLAUDE.md.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add GPU-accelerated bilateral filter via AGSL RuntimeShader
data.detail: The CPU bilateral filter was taking ~13 seconds, making the surface detail slider painfully slow. Replaced it with GPU-accelerated AGSL RuntimeShader running a two-pass separable bilateral (horizontal + vertical) with KERNEL_RADIUS=42, bringing it under 1 second. Depth values are encoded as grayscale ARGB_8888 bitmaps and passed via setInputBuffer to avoid color space conversion. The GPU filter is lazily initialized with eager shader compilation test, and DepthEnhancer now implements Closeable for proper GPU resource cleanup. Falls back to CPU silently on API < 33 or any GPU failure.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add 3D viewer with gallery, stereo display, and AR glasses support
data.detail: Major feature commit adding a full 3D viewing experience. Created StereoViewer composable as a reusable component that splits SBS images into left/right eye panes with synchronized zoom, pan, swipe navigation, and double-tap reset. Built ViewerScreen (third screen in app) with gallery grid browsing the SBS Converter output folder via MediaStore, swipe between images, delete with confirmation. Added Half SBS mode toggle for XREAL glasses with portrait/landscape detection, and eye swap toggle. Replaced the flat SBS preview in HomeScreen with the interactive StereoViewer. Fixed pair alignment export to use cross-eye format consistently. Images load at 4096px max to preserve 2048px per eye quality.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add convergence visualization overlay and arrangement toggle UI
data.detail: Added a real-time convergence visualization overlay that appears while the user drags the convergence slider. The overlay uses a Canvas composable that samples the normalized depth map at every 4th pixel and tints each region: blue for areas that will pop out of the screen (depth above convergence point), red for areas that will recede behind the screen, and yellow for the zero-parallax contour line. The ViewModel was updated to expose the normalized depth map as a StateFlow. The slider was wired up with onValueChange/onValueChangeFinished to track the drag state and show/hide the overlay accordingly. This gives users direct visual feedback about what their convergence setting will do before they regenerate the SBS image.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add settings UI, SBS preview, and save-to-gallery flow
data.detail: Extended the HomeScreen with full end-to-end SBS generation flow. Added SettingsSection with 3D Strength slider (0.5-8%) and Depth Smoothing slider (1-33 kernel), a Generate 3D button, SbsResultSection with preview image and generation time display, and a Save to Gallery button with saving state and success/failure feedback (auto-clears after 3 seconds). The ViewModel now caches raw depth maps to avoid re-inference when tweaking settings, properly recycles bitmaps to prevent OOM, and wires up SBS generation via ImageProcessor and gallery save via GalleryUtils. This completes the full user flow: pick image -> depth estimation -> adjust settings -> generate SBS -> save to gallery.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Read DATE_TAKEN from EXIF (not MediaStore) and write it to exported SBS images so they sort next to originals in gallery
data.detail: Photo picker returns content URIs that don't support MediaStore column queries, so EXIF DATETIME_ORIGINAL/DATETIME is used to extract the source photo's date. The date is written as both DATE_TAKEN and DATE_ADDED on the exported image's MediaStore entry. Applied to all three save paths: single, auto-3D batch, and pair-align batch.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Replace 256x256 mesh warp with GPU per-pixel backward warp shader for true per-pixel stereo precision
data.detail: Created GpuStereoWarper with a backward warp AGSL shader: each output pixel samples depth, computes shift (with convergence, gradient micro-parallax, and edge fade), then samples the source at the displaced position. Eliminates the 256x256 mesh vertex limit that was the CPU warp's resolution ceiling. CPU mesh warp kept as fallback for API < 33.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Photo picker content URIs don't support MediaStore column queries; must use EXIF stream parsing for date extraction
data.detail: The Android photo picker returns content:// URIs that fail when queried via MediaStore cursor for DATE_TAKEN. The workaround is opening an InputStream and parsing EXIF tags directly via ExifInterface. TAG_DATETIME_ORIGINAL is preferred, with TAG_DATETIME as fallback.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Create DepthAnalyzer utility for auto-parameter estimation from depth maps (not yet wired to UI)
data.detail: Added a standalone DepthAnalyzer object that computes recommended processing parameters from a normalized depth map. Uses Sobel edge density, Laplacian noise estimation, center-weighted depth mode for convergence, and stddev-based disparity scaling. Returns an AutoParams data class but is not yet connected to auto-set any sliders. This is the first version; the user later rejected auto-tuning entirely.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Switched from HuggingFace static FP16 ONNX export to fabio-sim dynamic export baked to fixed 518x518 FP32
data.detail: Replaced the HuggingFace community FP16 model (195MB, required NO_OPT) with the fabio-sim dynamic ONNX export baked to fixed 518x518 FP32 (371MB). The new model uses standard tensor names (image/depth), is compatible with BASIC_OPT, and removes the need to disable graph optimizations that the FP16 pre-fused nodes required.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Simplify SbsResult to just sbsBitmap, removing dead meshVerts/meshW/meshH fields
data.detail: With GPU per-pixel warp replacing mesh warp as the primary path, the mesh vertex data (meshVerts, meshW, meshH) in SbsResult was no longer needed for display. Simplified SbsResult to a single sbsBitmap field and removed mesh overlay references from HomeViewModel.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Upgrade depth model from 770x770 FP16 to 1022x1022 FP32 for maximum depth fidelity
data.detail: Switched to 1022x1022 (14x73 patches, nearest valid ViT multiple to 1024) which nearly doubles depth map pixel count. Reverted FP16 conversion -- model stays FP32 for quality. Accepted larger model size trade-off for finer depth detail.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Depth map statistics (stddev, edge density, noise level, median) can inform auto-parameter estimation for SBS generation
data.detail: Found that analyzing the normalized depth map with image processing techniques (Sobel gradients for edge density, Laplacian for noise, center-weighted histogram for convergence, stddev for spread) yields useful heuristics for setting 3D strength, blur kernel, gamma, and convergence automatically. However, this approach was later removed twice by user preference.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Preserve source photo date on exported SBS images
data.detail: Added date preservation to exported SBS images so they sort next to the original photos in the user's gallery. The key discovery was that Android's photo picker returns content URIs that don't support MediaStore column queries, requiring EXIF stream parsing instead. GalleryUtils gained a getDateTaken() method that reads TAG_DATETIME_ORIGINAL (falling back to TAG_DATETIME) from an InputStream. The saveBitmapToGallery method now accepts an optional dateTakenMs parameter and sets both DATE_TAKEN and DATE_ADDED on the MediaStore entry. All three save paths (HomeViewModel single export, BatchViewModel auto-3D, and BatchViewModel pair-align) were updated to pass the source date through. HomeViewModel also began tracking sourceUri to enable date lookup. Also removed DepthAnalyzer usage from BatchViewModel (replaced with default ProcessingConfig). 43 lines added, 26 removed across 3 files.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Added Python build script (prepare_model.py) to download, bake, and deploy ONNX model instead of direct curl in Gradle
data.detail: Created scripts/prepare_model.py that downloads the dynamic model from GitHub, uses onnxruntime.tools.make_dynamic_shape_fixed to bake it to 518x518, and copies it to assets. The Gradle prepareModel task invokes this script, replacing the old direct-curl approach. This enables shape baking and future model preprocessing steps.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Bias encoding for signed float data in AGSL: encode as (value/2*max)+0.5, decode as (sample*2-1)*scale
data.detail: Signed gradient values cannot be stored directly in unsigned 8-bit channels. Encode by normalizing to [-1,1] via max absolute value, then bias to [0,1] with +0.5. Shader reverses with (sample * 2.0 - 1.0) * gradientScale. gradientScale is passed as a uniform.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: fabio-sim ONNX export uses different tensor names (image/depth) vs HuggingFace (pixel_values/predicted_depth)
data.detail: The fabio-sim Depth Anything V2 ONNX export uses input tensor name "image" and output tensor name "depth", whereas the HuggingFace community export used "pixel_values" and "predicted_depth". This required updating the DepthEstimator's session.run() call accordingly.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: FP16 depth model was a dead end -- reverted to FP32 for quality; 1022 is nearest valid ViT patch multiple to 1024
data.detail: The FP16 conversion from commit 18410b8 was removed entirely. FP32 preserves full depth fidelity. 1022 = 14*73 is the closest valid ViT patch-aligned dimension to 1024, maximizing resolution within architecture constraints.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add DepthAnalyzer utility for auto-parameter estimation from depth maps
data.detail: Created a new DepthAnalyzer utility class that analyzes a normalized [0,1] depth map and returns recommended processing parameters (AutoParams). The analyzer computes: (1) basic statistics (mean, stddev, median, IQR), (2) Sobel gradient-based edge density to detect how much detail is in the depth map, (3) Laplacian-based noise estimation to decide blur kernel size, (4) center-weighted depth histogram mode for auto-convergence (assumes the subject is centered), and (5) stddev-based disparity scaling with an edge penalty. The gamma is computed to push the median depth toward 0.5 for balanced distribution. At this point the utility is standalone and not wired to any UI — it's a preparatory step. Notably, the user later rejected auto-tuning twice and had the DepthAnalyzer removed, preferring manual control with good defaults.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Replace hardcoded depth size values with DepthEstimator.MODEL_INPUT_SIZE constant across codebase
data.detail: GpuStereoWarper previously had a hardcoded 770 comment referencing the constant. Updated to use the actual constant. Other files (SbsWarper, BitmapUtils, HomeScreen) also updated to 1022.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Replace mesh warp with GPU per-pixel stereo warp via AGSL shader
data.detail: Replaced the CPU mesh-based stereo warp (limited to 256x256 vertices) with a GPU per-pixel backward warp shader using AGSL RuntimeShader. The shader takes source image, depth map, and optional gradient map as inputs, and for each output pixel computes the stereo displacement (with convergence, gradient micro-parallax, and edge fade) then samples the source at the displaced position. Signed gradient values are bias-encoded (0.5 = zero) to fit in unsigned 8-bit channels. SbsResult was simplified to just sbsBitmap since mesh data was no longer needed. Default surfaceDetail bumped from 0.5 to 1.0. CPU mesh warp retained as fallback for API < 33.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Removed DepthAnalyzer auto-tuning entirely; use fixed defaults (convergence 1.0, depthScale 5) with manual user control
data.detail: DepthAnalyzer's heuristics (center-weighted convergence, edge-density penalty, spread-factor scaling) didn't reliably improve results across varied inputs. Deleted the entire 111-line utility and changed convergencePoint default from 0.5 to 1.0. Users adjust manually via settings UI.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Switch depth model to fabio-sim dynamic ONNX export with build script
data.detail: Replaced the HuggingFace static FP16 ONNX model with the fabio-sim dynamic export, baked to fixed 518x518 input at FP32 precision. The old model (195MB FP16) had pre-fused SimplifiedLayerNormFusion nodes that forced NO_OPT in ONNX Runtime; the new model (371MB FP32) works with BASIC_OPT. Added scripts/prepare_model.py to automate the download-bake-deploy pipeline, which the Gradle prepareModel task now invokes instead of direct curl. Updated DepthEstimator to use the new tensor names (image/depth vs pixel_values/predicted_depth) and extracted MODEL_INPUT_SIZE as a companion constant. ModelManager was updated with the new filename. The size tradeoff (195MB -> 371MB) was accepted for better ONNX Runtime compatibility and standard graph optimization support.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Upgrade depth model to 1022x1022 FP32 for maximum depth fidelity
data.detail: Reversed the FP16 decision from commit 18410b8, prioritizing depth quality over model size. Upgraded from 770x770 FP16 (~188MB) to 1022x1022 FP32 (~376MB). The 1022 dimension was chosen as the nearest ViT-compatible patch multiple (14*73) to 1024, nearly doubling depth map pixel count from ~593K to ~1.04M. Updated all size references across 7 files: DepthEstimator, GpuStereoWarper (replaced hardcoded magic number with constant), SbsWarper, HomeScreen, BitmapUtils, ModelManager, and prepare_model.py. Removed the FP16 conversion step and onnxconverter-common dependency from the build pipeline.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: DepthAnalyzer auto-tuning heuristics failed to reliably improve results across varied depth inputs
data.detail: The auto-tuning approach used center-weighted histogram for convergence and Sobel edge density + depth stddev for scale. It was removed after testing showed it didn't generalize well. This was the second time DepthAnalyzer was rejected (user preference for manual control + good defaults).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Remove DepthAnalyzer and use fixed processing defaults
data.detail: Deleted the DepthAnalyzer utility (111 lines) which attempted to auto-tune convergence point and depth scale from depth map statistics. The heuristics -- center-weighted histogram mode for convergence, Sobel edge density and depth standard deviation for scale -- didn't reliably improve results across varied inputs. The convergencePoint default was changed from 0.5 to 1.0, and depthScale remains at 5.0. The user had previously rejected auto-tuning (this was the second removal), preferring manual control with good defaults via the settings UI. This aligns with the project's established preference against automatic parameter tuning.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Raw model depth output has more surface micro-detail than normalized/equalized depth; enhancement should extract from raw
data.detail: Histogram equalization and normalization flatten micro-detail in the depth map. By running the bilateral unsharp mask on the raw model output (full float32 range) and then applying the extracted detail onto the processed depth base, surface bumps, folds, and contours are preserved at much higher fidelity.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: MediaStore scanner runs when IS_PENDING flips to 0 and overwrites DATE_TAKEN from JPEG EXIF; must write EXIF before clearing pending
data.detail: When IS_PENDING is set to 0, Android's MediaStore scanner processes the file and derives DATE_TAKEN from the JPEG's EXIF DateTimeOriginal. Without EXIF tags, the scanner defaults to "now", overriding any DATE_TAKEN set in the initial ContentValues insert. The fix is a three-step process: write JPEG, write EXIF tags, then clear IS_PENDING.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Split bilateral unsharp mask to take raw depth + processed depth separately; extract detail from raw, apply to processed
data.detail: Changed bilateralUnsharpMask to accept both rawDepth (arbitrary float32 range from model) and processedDepth (normalized/equalized [0,1]). The bilateral filter runs on a locally-normalized version of the raw depth, detail is extracted as raw_normalized - smoothed, then applied as processed + strength * detail. depthSigma is auto-scaled to work with any raw value range.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Android bug: DATE_TAKEN returns null without SubSecDateTimeOriginal EXIF tag (Issue #385996093)
data.detail: There is a known Android bug where MediaStore DATE_TAKEN returns null if the JPEG lacks a SubSecDateTimeOriginal tag, even when DateTimeOriginal is present. Workaround is to write TAG_SUBSEC_TIME_ORIGINAL with a dummy value ("000") alongside the date tags.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Use RGBA_F16 for GPU bilateral filter input to avoid 8-bit quantization loss in depth encoding
data.detail: Switched floatArrayToBitmap from ARGB_8888 grayscale encoding to RGBA_F16 half-float encoding. This preserves full depth precision going into the shader. Output stays ARGB_8888 since the smoothed result only needs 8-bit precision for detail extraction against full float32 original.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Extract surface detail from raw depth for higher fidelity enhancement
data.detail: Discovered that the normalization and histogram equalization steps were flattening the micro-detail that the bilateral unsharp mask was trying to amplify. Refactored bilateralUnsharpMask to accept both raw model depth (full float32 range) and processed depth (normalized/equalized/gamma-corrected [0,1]). The bilateral filter now runs on locally-normalized raw depth to preserve the full dynamic range of the model's surface detail, then extracts detail = normalized_raw - smoothed and applies it to the processed depth base. The depthSigma is set at 0.04 on the normalized [0,1] range regardless of the raw value range. This approach captures surface micro-detail (bumps, folds, contours) that histogram equalization would otherwise flatten.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Three-step MediaStore save: (1) write JPEG, (2) write EXIF via file descriptor, (3) clear IS_PENDING with date re-assertion
data.detail: The correct order for saving images with preserved dates is: write JPEG data to the pending URI, open a read-write file descriptor to write EXIF tags (DateTimeOriginal, DateTimeDigitized, DateTime, timezone offset, subsecond), then clear IS_PENDING while re-asserting DATE_TAKEN/DATE_ADDED/DATE_MODIFIED in the same update() call.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix EXIF date preservation by writing date tags before clearing IS_PENDING
data.detail: The previous commit's date preservation approach failed because it only set DATE_TAKEN in the initial MediaStore ContentValues. When IS_PENDING was cleared to 0, the MediaStore scanner ran and overwrote DATE_TAKEN by reading the JPEG's EXIF -- which had no date tags since Bitmap.compress doesn't write EXIF. The fix restructured saveBitmapToGallery into a three-step process: (1) write JPEG data, (2) open a read-write file descriptor and write EXIF tags (DateTimeOriginal, DateTimeDigitized, DateTime, timezone offset via TAG_OFFSET_TIME_ORIGINAL, and a SubSecDateTimeOriginal workaround for Android bug #385996093), (3) clear IS_PENDING while re-asserting DATE_TAKEN, DATE_ADDED, and DATE_MODIFIED in the same update() call. Also cleaned up imports to use direct ExifInterface/SimpleDateFormat references. 45 lines added, 9 removed in GalleryUtils.kt.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Make depthSigma adaptive based on depth distribution stddev instead of fixed 0.04
data.detail: DepthEnhancer now calculates stddev of normalized depth and scales depthSigma from 0.02-0.08. Low stddev (distant scenes) gets finer sensitivity; high stddev (close-up) gets cleaner edge preservation. Formula: (0.02 + 0.04*stddev).coerceIn(0.01, 0.08).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Added timing instrumentation to GPU bilateral filter passes for performance monitoring
data.detail: Log.d timing for each stage: F16 encode, horizontal pass, vertical pass, F16 decode, and total time. Useful for diagnosing GPU performance regressions.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add GPU-accelerated bilateral filter via AGSL RuntimeShader
data.detail: Improved the GPU bilateral filter in two key ways. First, switched depth encoding from ARGB_8888 grayscale (8-bit quantization) to RGBA_F16 half-float for full precision going into the shader, using Half.toHalf for conversion. The output path keeps ARGB_8888 since smoothed depth only needs 8-bit for CPU detail extraction. Second, made depthSigma adaptive based on scene depth distribution: computes stddev of normalized depth and scales sigma between 0.02-0.08 so distant scenes get finer sensitivity and close-ups get cleaner edges. Added per-stage timing instrumentation to all GPU passes for performance monitoring.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Replace scroll layout and toggle buttons with TabRow navigation and auto-generation on slider release
data.detail: Replaced the scrollable layout and floating toggle buttons with a Material3 TabRow for switching between Depth Compare, SBS Result, and Wigglegram views. Removed the manual "Generate 3D" button in favor of automatic SBS generation after depth estimation and debounced on slider release via onSliderFinished(). SBS result auto-switches to the SBS Result tab. Advanced settings (gamma, smoothing, arrangement) collapsed behind an expandable section.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Remove DepthAnalyzer utility and mesh wireframe overlay — simplifying to manual controls only
data.detail: Deleted DepthAnalyzer.kt entirely (first removal) and removed the MeshWireframeOverlay composable plus associated mesh toggle states. This reflects a preference for manual user control over auto-tuning and removes debug visualization features that weren't useful to end users.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Tall images overflow: aspectRatio must use matchHeightConstraintsFirst=true to prevent clipping
data.detail: Tall/portrait images caused layout overflow because Compose's aspectRatio defaults to matching width constraints. Adding matchHeightConstraintsFirst=true to all aspectRatio calls ensures the image fits within the available height, preventing tall images from being clipped or causing scroll issues in the fixed-height layout.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Use Android Presentation API with custom PresentationLifecycleOwner for AR glasses output instead of XREAL SDK
data.detail: XREAL SDK is Unity-only, so glasses are treated as a USB-C DisplayPort monitor. GlassesPresentation extends android.app.Presentation and renders StereoViewer in Half SBS mode on the external display. Custom PresentationLifecycleOwner provides LifecycleOwner, SavedStateRegistryOwner, and ViewModelStoreOwner required by ComposeView on its ViewTree.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: ComposeView in Presentation requires three ViewTree owners manually set on DecorView: Lifecycle, SavedState, ViewModelStore
data.detail: Android Presentation doesn't provide LifecycleOwner by default. ComposeView walks up the view hierarchy looking for all three owners. Must set them on window.decorView using setViewTreeLifecycleOwner, setViewTreeSavedStateRegistryOwner, and setViewTreeViewModelStoreOwner.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Streamline UI with tab navigation, auto-generation, and collapsible advanced settings
data.detail: Major UI streamlining. The scrollable layout with floating toggle buttons was replaced with a TabRow-based navigation between three view modes: Depth Compare (reveal slider), SBS Result, and Wigglegram. The manual "Generate 3D" button was removed — SBS generation now happens automatically after depth estimation completes and on slider release via a debounced onSliderFinished() callback. The generated result auto-switches to the SBS Result tab. Advanced settings (Depth Contrast/gamma, Edge Smoothing, Arrangement toggle) were collapsed behind an expandable section with AnimatedVisibility, keeping the primary 3D Strength and Convergence sliders always visible. ProcessingConfig gained a smoothingLabel property for human-readable blur kernel descriptions. The DepthAnalyzer utility was deleted (first of two removals) along with the MeshWireframeOverlay composable — both considered unnecessary for the user-facing experience. All aspectRatio calls were updated with matchHeightConstraintsFirst=true to fix tall image overflow. The ViewModel gained a ViewMode enum and cancellable auto-generate job with isActive check to properly discard stale results.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Auto-detect external displays via DisplayManager listener, toggle glasses output from viewer toolbar
data.detail: ViewerViewModel registers a DisplayManager.DisplayListener to detect glasses connect/disconnect. Glasses button only shows when external display is present. LaunchedEffect syncs image and swapEyes to glasses when active.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add AR glasses support via Presentation API with custom LifecycleOwner
data.detail: Implemented external display output for XREAL AR glasses using Android's Presentation API. Created GlassesPresentation (136 lines) that renders the StereoViewer composable in Half SBS mode at native glasses resolution. The key challenge was that Presentation doesn't provide LifecycleOwner, but ComposeView requires LifecycleOwner + SavedStateRegistryOwner + ViewModelStoreOwner on its view tree ancestors. Solved with PresentationLifecycleOwner that implements all three interfaces and is set on the DecorView. ViewerViewModel gained display detection via DisplayManager listener and glasses toggle/dismiss/sync methods. The viewer toolbar shows a glasses button only when external display is detected. Phone retains controls while glasses show immersive 3D. Default convergence changed from 1.0 to 0.5.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-add DepthAnalyzer (second version) to auto-tune 3D strength and convergence from depth stats
data.detail: Re-introduced a slimmed-down DepthAnalyzer that only outputs depthScale and convergencePoint (removed blur/gamma auto-tuning from v1). Wired it into runDepthEstimation to auto-set those two config values after every depth estimation. This is the second time DepthAnalyzer was added — the user later removed it again, preferring manual control with good defaults.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: DepthAnalyzer keeps getting re-added and removed — user rejects auto-tuning in favor of manual controls
data.detail: This is the second time DepthAnalyzer was introduced (first in commit 21b3bd5, removed in 908bab3, re-added here). Per memory notes, the user removed it twice. The project constraint is clear: the user prefers manual control with good defaults over any automated parameter adjustment, even when the analysis is technically sound.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add DepthAnalyzer for auto-tuning 3D strength and convergence from depth stats
data.detail: Re-introduced the DepthAnalyzer utility for the second time, this time as a slimmed-down version that only computes depthScale and convergencePoint (the v1 in commit 21b3bd5 also computed blur kernel and gamma). The analyzer uses center-weighted depth histogram for convergence and stddev/edge-density-based scaling for 3D strength. It was wired into HomeViewModel.runDepthEstimation to auto-set sliders immediately after depth inference. However, per project memory, this auto-tuning approach was rejected by the user twice — they prefer manual control with sensible defaults. This commit represents the recurring tension between automation and user agency in the project.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Sync phone zoom/pan gestures to glasses via onTransformChanged callback and external state parameters
data.detail: StereoViewer now supports external state (externalScale/OffsetX/OffsetY) that overrides internal gesture state. When externally controlled, gesture handling is disabled. Phone viewer reports transforms via onTransformChanged, which ViewerViewModel forwards to GlassesPresentation.updateTransform.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix Half SBS: use ContentScale.Fit + scaleX*0.5 instead of FillBounds/FillHeight for universal aspect ratio support
data.detail: Previous approach used FillBounds for landscape and FillHeight for portrait halves, which was fragile. New approach always uses ContentScale.Fit and applies scaleX=scale*0.5 in Half SBS mode, pre-squeezing horizontally by 50% so glasses' 2x unsqueeze restores correct aspect ratio on all source images. Removed isPortraitHalf parameter entirely.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add FilterQuality.High to BitmapPainters for smoother downscaling in stereo viewer
data.detail: BitmapPainter now uses FilterQuality.High for both left and right eye painters, providing bilinear filtering for smoother image display especially when zoomed out.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Sync zoom/pan gestures to glasses and add bilinear filtering
data.detail: Made the phone-to-glasses viewing experience fully synchronized. StereoViewer was refactored to support dual modes: internal gesture state (phone) and external state (glasses). Added externalScale/OffsetX/OffsetY parameters that override internal state when provided, and onTransformChanged callback that reports every gesture update. When externally controlled, pointer input is disabled. GlassesPresentation gained updateTransform method with mutableStateOf for each axis. ViewerViewModel bridges the two via updateGlassesTransform. Also fixed Half SBS rendering: replaced the fragile FillBounds/FillHeight approach with ContentScale.Fit + scaleX*0.5, which correctly pre-squeezes for glasses unsqueeze on all aspect ratios, eliminating the portrait/landscape special cases. Added FilterQuality.High to BitmapPainters for bilinear filtering.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pivoted from Flutter to native Android/Kotlin+Compose after PRD review
data.detail: User initially asked for a Flutter project. Assistant scaffolded Flutter files, but upon reading the updated PRD, discovered it specified "Pure native Android, no Flutter." The PRD's architecture (ONNX Runtime, MediaCodec, EGL encoding) was very Android-native and would require significant platform channel work in Flutter. User agreed to scrap Flutter and go native.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Bundle depth model in APK rather than download-on-launch for simplicity
data.detail: Two options were discussed: download on first launch (~195MB) requiring hosting (Google Drive, GitHub LFS), or bundle in APK. User chose bundling since it's a personal-use tool. Simpler implementation, no hosting needed, but large APK size accepted as tradeoff.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Chose MediaCodec over FFmpegKit for video I/O because FFmpeg is no longer maintained
data.detail: User was given the choice between MediaCodec (Option A in PRD) and FFmpegKit (Option B as fallback). User explicitly chose MediaCodec because "ffmpeg isnt maintained anymore." This rules out FFmpegKit as a future escape hatch for video processing.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: OOM crash on Pixel 10 was two distinct bugs: full-res 50MP bitmap loading AND recycled bitmap lifecycle from asImageBitmap() wrapping
data.detail: Initial crash was 200MB bitmap from Pixel camera (no inSampleSize downsampling). After fixing that, a second crash appeared on small 1.1MP images: Bitmap.asImageBitmap() wraps the original bitmap (doesn't copy), so calling recycle() on the backing Bitmap while Compose still references the ImageBitmap causes a "trying to use a recycled bitmap" crash. Fix: keep backing bitmaps alive, only recycle the previous one when replaced.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Start with Canvas-based warping but use mesh approach (drawBitmapMesh) for hole filling
data.detail: User was given choice between Canvas (simpler/slower) and OpenGL (faster/more code) for SBS warp. Chose Canvas for v1 but insisted on mesh-based warping for better hole filling. Discovery during research: Android's Canvas.drawBitmapMesh() is purpose-built for this, eliminating need for manual triangle rasterization.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Mesh warp looked flat despite detailed depth map because min-max normalization compresses 90% of depth values into narrow range
data.detail: The depth map visually showed excellent detail (hair, shoulders, contours) but the mesh overlay was nearly uniform with only edge warping. Root cause: min-max normalization clusters within-object depth variations into a narrow disparity range. Histogram equalization was the key fix — it redistributes depth values uniformly so the full disparity range is used, making the warp displacement match the visual depth detail.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: DepthAnalyzer removed a third time -- user wants manual control with convergence default 100%
data.detail: DepthAnalyzer was re-added at user's request (scoped to only strength+convergence), then removed again in the same session. This is the third removal cycle. User explicitly asked to default convergence to 100% instead of auto-computed values. The pattern is definitive: no auto-tuning, ever.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Unified gesture handling into single awaitEachGesture block to resolve competing gesture detectors
data.detail: Two separate pointerInput blocks (detectTransformGestures and tap detector) were fighting over gesture ownership in StereoViewer. Swipe navigation broke because the transform gesture consumed all drags. The fix: merge everything into a single awaitEachGesture block that handles double-tap zoom, single-finger swipe (at 1x), single-finger pan (when zoomed), and multi-finger zoom/pan. Swipe threshold is 15% of screen width.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: User rejects auto-tuning features — removed DepthAnalyzer twice, prefers manual sliders with good defaults
data.detail: User initially asked about auto-best-settings from depth analysis, then interrupted the request. After DepthAnalyzer was built and wired into auto-setting sliders, user immediately asked to revert auto-slider changes. Later when a UX plan included DepthAnalyzer, user explicitly said "don't use the depth analyzer, I asked you to remove that earlier, you can delete it." Strong pattern: user wants manual control with well-chosen defaults, not auto-tuning.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Batch processing feature motivated by real-world Carcassonne trip photos -- user needed mass conversion
data.detail: The batch processing screen was built because the user had a large collection of photos from a trip to Carcassonne that needed converting to 3D. This drove the queue-based architecture with auto depth analysis per image, inline SBS result previews, and end-to-end export without manual intervention per photo.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shared ONNX model via SbsApplication to avoid re-initialization across Home and Batch screens
data.detail: When adding the batch screen, the depth model was moved to SbsApplication (Application subclass) so both HomeViewModel and BatchViewModel share the same ONNX session. This avoids expensive model re-initialization when switching between screens.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Gallery grid view is now the default entry point for View button, not direct image viewer
data.detail: User requested a gallery view as the first screen when tapping View. 2-column grid of thumbnails at 512px using only the left half of each SBS image. Single image viewer accessible by tapping a thumbnail. Gallery supports multi-select via long-press for batch deletion. Back navigation: image viewer -> gallery -> main screen.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Cross-eye view with convergence point adjustment was the key to making 3D output look convincing, inspired by Scopi app comparison
data.detail: User reported that depth "didn't look right" in the app but looked "perfect and amazing" in Scopi with cross-eye view. Research revealed convergence point adjustment (zero-parallax plane) was the #1 missing feature. With convergence at ~50%, objects split into pop-out (closer) and recede (farther) relative to screen plane — matching how real stereo cameras work. Cross-eye mode swaps L/R halves in the output bitmap, baked into exports.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Chose BoofCV for stereo pair alignment over OpenCV -- pure Java, no NDK, sequential filename pairing
data.detail: For the pair alignment batch mode, BoofCV was chosen because it's pure Java (no native NDK needed). Pairs are matched by sequential filenames. The BoofCV 1.1.7 API had significant differences from documentation, requiring manual brute-force matching instead of FastAccess-based association. Multiple API mismatches were encountered and worked around.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Modifier.align() only works on direct children of Box layout, not through wrapper composables
data.detail: Delete button was invisible because Modifier.align(BottomCenter) was placed inside a TooltipBox wrapper, but align only works on direct Box children. The TooltipBox was the direct child, positioned at default top-left, hiding the button behind the back button. Fix: wrap each control in a Box(modifier=Modifier.align(...)) first, then place TooltipBox inside.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: User's preferred defaults after testing: strength 5%, contrast 1.0, smoothing 3, convergence 50%, cross-eye enabled
data.detail: After extensive manual testing with real photos, user settled on these defaults as producing the best results: 3D strength 5% (very subtle), depth contrast 1.0 (no histogram equalization boost), edge smoothing 3, convergence 50% (subject at screen plane), cross-eye mode enabled. User exclaimed "it looks SO GOOD" after these settings.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Edge fade ramps warp shift to zero at the trailing edge of each eye (2% border) to eliminate black pixel artifacts
data.detail: User identified that SBS warp creates black borders where pixels shift away from the edge. Initially the assistant got the edge sides backwards — left eye shifts pixels left, so the RIGHT edge loses pixels (trailing edge), not the left. The fade is directional: only applied to the single trailing edge per eye, not all sides. 2% inward ramp from zero to full shift.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Edge fade direction: left eye fades RIGHT edge, right eye fades LEFT edge — initially implemented backwards
data.detail: The intuition of "pulling away from" vs "shifting toward" is confusing. Left eye shifts pixels LEFT, so the RIGHT edge is where pixels are pulled AWAY from, creating black gaps. This was implemented incorrectly on the first attempt, and the user corrected it. The direction convention is: the fade applies to the TRAILING edge — opposite to the shift direction.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: 512x512 mesh density caused app stalls when rendering wireframe overlay — reverted to 256 max with MESH_DIVISOR=8
data.detail: Increasing mesh density to 512x512 for better warp quality produced ~1M vertices for the wireframe overlay to draw, causing visible UI stalls. The warp quality difference between 256 and 512 was marginal since the depth map was only 518x518 at the time. The real quality improvement came from the depth remapping pipeline (histogram equalization), not mesh density. Reverted to MESH_DIVISOR=8, MAX_MESH_DIM=256.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Removed Half SBS output option; Full SBS is the only output format needed
data.detail: Initially implemented both Half SBS and Full SBS with UI toggle chips. User tested and decided Half SBS is unnecessary — Full SBS (2x width, no horizontal compression) is all that's needed. Removed OutputFormat enum and UI chips to simplify.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Compose aspectRatio modifier must use matchHeightConstraintsFirst=true to prevent tall images from overflowing parent height
data.detail: In the single-screen layout with weight(1f) for the image area, Compose's aspectRatio modifier defaults to matching width first, which pushes tall images beyond the available height, overlapping top buttons. Fix: add matchHeightConstraintsFirst=true to all aspectRatio calls on image containers (DepthRevealSlider Box, SBS preview, etc.) so they shrink to fit vertically.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Emoji icons (e.g. trash can) may not render on all Android devices; Canvas-drawn icons are more reliable
data.detail: User reported the trash can emoji was invisible in the viewer. A Canvas-drawn TrashCanIcon composable was built as replacement. However, user then asked to go back to emoji with proper rendering (white color, correct positioning). The real issue turned out to be layout positioning (align bug), not emoji rendering. Lesson: verify the actual problem before building alternative solutions.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: BoofCV 1.1.7 API mismatches -- FactoryAssociation.greedy and FastAccess types differed from docs
data.detail: BoofCV 1.1.7's FactoryAssociation.greedy takes (ConfigAssociateGreedy?, ScoreAssociation) not (ScoreAssociation, boolean). The setSource/setDestination methods expect FastAccess not List. Workaround: implemented manual brute-force descriptor matching with a custom descriptorDistance helper, bypassing the association API entirely.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: CPU bilateral filter took ~13 seconds at 770x770 full res — spatialSigma=21 gives radius 42, ~101M multiply-adds per frame
data.detail: The pure CPU bilateral filter with nested for loops running on Dispatchers.Default was far too slow for interactive use. Half-resolution (378x378) brought it to ~3s but user rejected the quality tradeoff. This drove the decision to move to AGSL GPU shaders, which brought it to under 1 second.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Initial stereo alignment only warped right image, causing zoom mismatch on askew photos
data.detail: First alignment implementation only transformed the right image to match the left. When photos had significant rotation/zoom differences, this produced visible zoom mismatches. Fixed by applying the transform symmetrically -- half the correction to each image so they meet in the middle, analogous to symmetric depth warping.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: UI redesigned to zero-click generation with tab navigation (Original/3D Result/Wigglegram) and auto-regeneration on slider release only
data.detail: Major UX overhaul: removed the Generate 3D button entirely — SBS now auto-generates after depth estimation and regenerates when sliders change. User specifically requested auto-compute only triggers on slider release (onValueChangeFinished), not during drag. Added tab navigation replacing hidden toggles, collapsed advanced settings by default, recovered ~130dp for image preview. Mesh overlay feature was also removed at user's request after initial implementation.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: ONNX Runtime ALL_OPT optimization crashes with HuggingFace FP16 model
data.detail: The FP16 model from HuggingFace failed to load with ALL_OPT graph optimization — error referenced "InsertedPrecisionFreeCast" nodes that the runtime couldn't resolve. Fix was to disable graph optimizations (NO_OPT). This is why ONNX optimization levels were later treated cautiously.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Surface Detail slider only recomputes depth preview (fast), not full 3D — lazy SBS regen triggers on tab switch via dirty flag
data.detail: User explicitly requested that Surface Detail slider adjustment should NOT trigger full 3D regeneration for faster iteration. Instead, a depthDirty flag tracks when depth has changed since last SBS generation. Switching to SBS Result or Wigglegram tabs checks this flag and auto-regenerates only if dirty. This allows rapid depth preview tweaking without waiting for warp+composite each time.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: RANSAC alignment used only 2 inlier points for refit -- caused severe misalignment on rotated pairs
data.detail: The initial RANSAC implementation refit the model using only 2 inlier points (line 238) instead of all inliers via least-squares. Combined with only 200 iterations, 512px alignment resolution, and no stereo constraint (no vertical disparity filtering), this caused severe failures on images with significant rotation. Fixed with least-squares solver using ALL inliers, 500+ iterations, higher resolution, and epipolar filtering.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Histogram equalization amplifies background depth variations beyond physically realistic levels, making far objects appear to have too much parallax
data.detail: Depth Anything V2 outputs inverse depth (1/Z) which naturally follows correct stereo relationship. Histogram equalization redistributes values uniformly, maintaining ordering but changing spacing — this means background depth variations that were originally tiny get amplified to use a proportional chunk of the disparity range. User set contrast to 1.0 (minimal equalization) as a result, preferring more natural depth distribution.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Half SBS mode detects portrait vs landscape: FillBounds for landscape, FillHeight for portrait halves
data.detail: User reported H (Half SBS) button worked for horizontal images but made vertical images worse on XREAL glasses. For landscape half-images, FillBounds correctly squeezes horizontally. For portrait half-images, FillBounds also stretches vertically (wrong). Fix: detect aspect ratio per half-image and use FillHeight for portrait (fills pane height, natural horizontal squeeze). Later replaced entirely by the universal ContentScale.Fit + scaleX*0.5 approach.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Surface detail preview appeared non-functional due to async timing: coroutine hadn't completed before isAdjusting flag was cleared
data.detail: When releasing the Surface Detail slider, onSurfaceDetailFinished() launched a coroutine for refreshEnhancedDepthPreview, but onSurfaceDetailAdjusting(false) was called immediately after, snapping the view back to SBS_RESULT before the coroutine completed. Fix was to have refreshEnhancedDepthPreview itself force the view to DEPTH_COMPARE mode, decoupled from the adjusting flag.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Output tensor cast error: ONNX depth output is float[][][] (3D), not float[][] (2D)
data.detail: Initial code assumed output shape [518,518] as 2D array. Actual Java representation of [1,518,518] tensor is float[][][] (Array<Array<FloatArray>>). Got ClassCastException "float[][][] cannot be cast to float[][]" at runtime. Plan review caught this but the initial fix was still wrong — had to debug on-device to get the correct cast.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Two-stage alignment: rotation+scale first, crop black borders, then translation-only refinement
data.detail: Cropping black borders after alignment shifted image registration. User proposed a two-stage approach: first pass does full similarity transform (rotation+scale), crop removes black borders, second pass does translation-only refinement (median of vertical displacements only). Horizontal displacement is preserved as it's the stereo parallax that creates depth.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Convergence visualization (blue/red/yellow overlay) auto-switches to original/depth view while slider is being dragged
data.detail: The convergence overlay maps to depth image dimensions, so it must display over the depth/original reveal slider, not the SBS result. When the user drags the convergence slider, the preview automatically switches to DEPTH_COMPARE mode showing the two-tone tint (blue=pops out, red=recedes, yellow=screen plane contour). On release, it returns to the previous tab. This was a refinement requested by the user after initial implementation.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: SBS viewer resolution bug: loadBitmapFromUri caps WHOLE image at 2048px, then splits, giving only 1024px per eye
data.detail: User noticed viewer looked low-res compared to Google Photos. Root cause: loadBitmapFromUri caps the entire SBS image at 2048px on longest edge. For a 4096x2048 SBS image, this means each eye only gets 1024px. Fix: added optional maxDimension parameter, viewer uses 4096px cap to preserve 2048px per eye. Other callers (depth processing, batch) keep default 2048px cap.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: FP16 quantization of fabio-sim dynamic model produces broken Cast nodes regardless of keep_io_types setting
data.detail: Attempted to quantize fabio-sim's FP32 dynamic model to FP16 using onnxconverter-common. Both keep_io_types=True and keep_io_types=False produced models with type mismatches in Cast nodes. Error: "Type (tensor(float16)) of output arg does not match expected type (tensor(float))." The HuggingFace FP16 worked because their team used a different quantization pipeline. Settled on using FP32 directly.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Depth Anything V2 requires input sizes that are multiples of 14 (ViT patch size) — 1024 is invalid, closest are 1022 or 1036
data.detail: User requested 1024 depth resolution. ViT-B uses 14x14 patches, so input must be multiple of 14. 1024/14=73.14 is not integer. Valid options: 1022 (14*73), 1036 (14*74). User first requested 768 which is also invalid (768/14=54.86), closest valid is 770 (14*55). The model was baked at 770 initially, later upgraded to 1022 for maximum fidelity.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: 756x756 depth model works on Pixel 10 after proper memory management — model was already cached from earlier attempt
data.detail: User wanted higher depth fidelity despite previous failed attempts. The 756x756 model had already been built and cached in a prior session. The real blocker wasn't the model size but the lack of image downsampling on load (50MP at full res). After adding inSampleSize-based downsampling and fixing bitmap lifecycle, the 756 model runs without OOM. User confirmed the extra fidelity "means a lot more accuracy in the final output."
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: 756x756 FP32 depth model OOMs on Android emulator after generating depth map
data.detail: The 756x756 model worked correctly (produced good depth maps) but the app was killed by Android's lowmemorykiller after inference completed. 371MB model + inference activations at higher resolution exceeded emulator heap. Fell back to 518x518 FP32. Noted that real devices with more RAM would likely handle 756x756 fine.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Translation-only second pass must correct ONLY vertical shift -- horizontal displacement is stereo parallax
data.detail: In stereo pair alignment, horizontal differences between left and right images are the parallax that creates the 3D effect. The second-pass refinement after cropping must only correct vertical shift (median of vertical displacements). Correcting horizontal shift would eliminate the depth perception.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Removed test image selection from UI — real photos from device gallery are the only input source now
data.detail: User explicitly requested removing test image selection in the first message of the session, stating "we don't need that anymore, we can just load images from the phone." This was part of the broader UI redesign to fit everything on one screen. The test images were development artifacts that cluttered the production UI.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pixel 10 ImageReader rejects PixelFormat.RGBA_F16 (format 22) for GPU render targets
data.detail: Attempted to upgrade GPU bilateral filter from ARGB_8888 to RGBA_F16 for higher precision depth processing. AGSL shader computes in float internally, but ImageReader on Pixel 10 throws 'Invalid format specified 22' for F16 render targets. Fallback to CPU bilateral took ~13 seconds. Resolution: keep F16 for input bitmap only (shader reads at full half-float precision), use 8-bit for ImageReader output and intermediate passes. Smoothed output only needs 8-bit since detail extraction uses full float32 original.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: ONNX Runtime ALL_OPT and EXTENDED_OPT both produce invalid graph ordering on baked Depth Anything models — nodes reference predecessors not yet defined
data.detail: When using ALL_OPT or EXTENDED_OPT session options with the baked 770x770 model, ONNX Runtime produced 'Invalid model: Node input is not a graph input, initializer, or output of a previous node' errors. The fix_output_shapes step in prepare_model.py and the transformer optimizer both produced invalid node ordering. Only BASIC_OPT worked reliably at runtime. The solution was to pre-optimize offline with onnxruntime.transformers.optimizer and use BASIC_OPT at runtime.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: App crashes with OOM when regenerating SBS images due to bitmap leak
data.detail: Each SBS generation created a new Full SBS bitmap (~96MB for 6144x4080 at 4 bytes/pixel) without recycling the previous one. After a few regenerations, the app would close. Fixed by explicitly calling recycle() on old SBS and depth bitmaps before creating new ones in HomeViewModel.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Cross-eye vs parallel format confusion -- preview was double-swapping, making export appear flat
data.detail: Pair alignment was set to export cross-eye format, but the ResultPreview composable was swapping halves of the already-cross-eye export, turning it back to parallel. This made the preview look 3D (accidental correct format) while the export file appeared flat. After multiple iterations, settled on parallel export format with no preview swapping -- both match and cross-eye viewing works correctly.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Material3 extended icons (e.g. GridOn) not available without extra dependency — must use Canvas-drawn custom icons or default set only
data.detail: During mesh overlay implementation, Icons.Default.GridOn was not available in the base material-icons package (it's in the extended set). Rather than adding the extended icons dependency, the solution was to draw a custom grid icon via Canvas composable. This constraint applies to any icon not in the default material3 icon set.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: XNNPACK execution provider is incompatible with Depth Anything V2 ONNX model — causes graph validity errors and contention warnings
data.detail: XNNPACK was tried as an ONNX Runtime accelerator. It loaded but: (1) produced thread pool contention warnings requiring ORT threads set to 1, (2) ALL_OPT + XNNPACK caused the model init to hang indefinitely, (3) even after fixing threads, XNNPACK reordered nodes causing graph validity errors on different layers. XNNPACK was ultimately removed entirely. The working config is: pre-optimized model + BASIC_OPT + 6 CPU threads, no XNNPACK.
data.author: Colton Dyck
